svg.text: KHMER + MON — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 58D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. KHMER PROVERB — ON WATER —
តឹកច្រឹនច្រែនតែញ កុម្បាលមិនទឹកតឹកជីវិតជានច្រឹនជីវិតន៉ូក
Tịk chăroen chēn tēy, kumpal min tịk jīvit jān chăroen jīvit noek.
"Water flowing downstream never stops; cannot stop the river of life either." (Khmer proverb)
— II. ANGKOR WAT INSCRIPTION — ON TIME (~12TH C. CE) —
Yasaṭha nirîyamānam pīṇḍa māṇatvā sarvam etad iti cetasi...
Sanskrit: “Knowing all this to be impermanent in one’s heart...” — Angkor inscription fragment.
Khmer inscriptions often mixed Sanskrit with Khmer — the language of the gods alongside the language of the people.
— III. KHMER BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
នេហ័កនេហក្រមបានជាសម្រាប់អ្នកតើតម្លេចជាមនេហកសូម តឹកាន់ចង។ជាមានកាលព្រែ
Nēh baan jā samrāp anak tē tmel jaa mnēh ksūm · tīkāŋ chan jām kāl phrēy
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Khmer)
— KHMER NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Anakot (យកកាល) = future (from Sanskrit anāgata “not yet come”). Chan jām = remember.
Sanskrit loanwords dominate Khmer abstract vocabulary — the legacy of Angkor’s Hindu-Buddhist civilization.
SECONDARY — MON — RUNNING TEXT
— I. MON PROVERB — ON MEMORY AND RIVERS —
Kruī lɔp pā ceikā, miɔ lɔp pā yōm.
River flows to sea person flows to memory.
"The river flows to the sea; a person flows into memory." (Mon proverb on the persistence of the dead in memory)
— II. MON INSCRIPTION — WAT MAHEYONG (~11TH C. CE) —
Restored Mon text: “By the merit of this deed, may all sentient beings attain peace.”
Mon temple inscriptions (~11th–13th c.) are among the oldest texts of mainland Southeast Asia.
— III. MON BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Nɛ krɔ miɔ klɛh samrāp cāŋ, dot krāk,
yōm miɔ patɔn hɔn.
This by people made for you freely / remember person future also.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Mon)
— MON NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Patɔn = future/ahead (lit. the forward path). Yōm = to remember, to keep in heart.
Dot krāk = freely/without cost (lit. without price). Mon preserves archaic Austroasiatic vocabulary.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — A PARENT AND ITS CHILD —
Mon: ~800K speakers. Endangered. Once the dominant culture of mainland SE Asia. Ancestor of Khmer script.
Khmer: ~16M speakers. Official language of Cambodia. Inherited its script from Mon. Now the larger language.
History reversed: the child-script (Khmer) now belongs to a larger community than the parent (Mon).
This plate pairs them as equals — a reminder that cultural inheritance runs in all directions.
PLATE 58D ALT · Khmer / Mon · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
